response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile engineer, but his profile misses every mandatory pillar of this role. His professional C++ exposure amounts to roughly seven months at National Instruments in 2014-2015, far short of the required 5-10 years of hands-on Windows C++. His database work centers on MySQL and MongoDB with no stated SQL Server or Oracle experience, and he has no fixed income, derivatives, or capital-markets domain background — RIDE Capital was a Node/TypeScript fintech role, not investment banking. His recent decade is dominated by D, TypeScript, and Node.js web platforms, making him a poor match for a specialized capital-markets C++ consulting engagement.
strengths: Strong systems-programming fundamentals, including low-latency, high-throughput services (millions of requests/sec) at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic
Solid engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and DevOps tooling experience
Broad polyglot background (D, C/C++, Java, TypeScript, Node.js) with demonstrated ability to learn new stacks quickly
concerns: Only ~7 months of professional C++ experience, and it dates back to 2014-2015, versus the mandatory 5-10 years on Windows
No SQL Server or Oracle experience listed; database background is MySQL/MongoDB
No fixed income, derivatives, or capital-markets/investment-banking domain experience

key_factors.factor: No fixed income / capital-markets domain background
Strong general engineering and low-latency systems skills
